rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA CELIA HERNANDEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. petitioner must bring a proposed order to the hearing. that order must have the legal description of the real property (NOT just the address) in the body of the order, NOT as an attachment. in addition, petitioner may be asked whether either he or his brother have ever been incarcerated as the petition alleges both that notice to the Victim's Compensaton Board is and is not required, under Probate code section 9202(b), and was and was not given. finally, Orlando filed a Renunciation of Inheritance on 6/24/19 but the petition asks the property be ordered 50/50 to petiitoner and orlando. Petitioner may be asked to explain.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDIA M. MCGREW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/18/20. Amended Examiner notes emailed 1/16/20 at 11:46am. Issues: 1) Please explain under what legal authority distribution of deceased attorney Francis J. Kelly’s share of statutory fees goes to Sylvia C. Kelly as Trustee. A certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days, the original 13100 affidavit, a copy of the acceptance of trusteeship, or a certified copy of the Spousal Property Order must be filed. (LRSF 14.67). 2) The ending balance of cash in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. (LRSF 14.62).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/11/20. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] The list of persons attached to the petitioner's Second Supplement filed on 12/5/19 has not yet been updated in a verified third supplement to the petition that identifies all of the prior, deceased ancestors of those parties listed and to show their relationship to the decedent. Identifying parties as "first cousin, once removed" on a service list is not sufficient. An endorsed filed copy of the third supplement must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/4/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT H FONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/18/20. Prior to review of the petition, the following must be filed (at least 2 weeks prior to continued hearing date): 1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, with completed Proof of Service 2) Real Property Declaration, as required by Local Rules 3) Duties and Liabilities In addition, a completed proposed Order for Probate must be submitted to Room 202 (at least 2 weeks prior to the continued hearing date). Please note that the Court can only correspond with the petitioner, unless the petitioner is represented by an attorney.
